key_insight: Data-efficient fine-tuning of a foundation ML interatomic potential can reproduce ammonium sulfate’s barocaloric phase transformation with only 5–10 DFT configurations.
engineering_relevance: This lowers the DFT data burden for screening and simulating solid-state cooling materials, making atomistic discovery of barocaloric candidates more practical.
method: Train MACE-based MLIPs from scratch or fine-tune the MACE-MPA-0 foundation model
Compare naive replay vs multihead replay fine-tuning across multiple training-set sizes
Test each model in molecular dynamics by checking whether the temperature-driven structural phase transition is recovered
evidence_signal: Across training-set sizes, fine-tuned models reproduced the phase transformation, while the foundation model alone and many from-scratch models failed; the study also reports that hybrid-DFT training and dispersion correction are needed for correct phase behavior.
watch_item: The result is demonstrated on a prototypical single material system, so transferability to other barocaloric compounds and other phase-change chemistries still needs validation.
paper_title: Barocaloric phase transformation from data efficient fine-tuning of machine learned interatomic potentials
